questions.explanation: Wastewater is treated in sewage treatment plants (STP) to remove pollutants before releasing into water bodies.

questions.text: Sludge is the solid waste that settles at the bottom during wastewater treatment.
questions.explanation: Sludge is the solid organic matter that settles at the bottom during sedimentation in wastewater treatment.

questions.correctAnswer: Gas produced by anaerobic decomposition of organic matter, mainly methane.
questions.explanation: Biogas is produced when sludge decomposes anaerobically. It contains methane and can be used as fuel.

questions.text: The process of removing pollutants from water before it enters water bodies is called ________.
questions.explanation: Sewage treatment is the process of removing contaminants from wastewater to make it safe for disposal.
